contributor: university of illinois at urbana-champaign library, urbana, il
description: ORIGINAL POETRY FOR THE GENIUS OF LIBERTV PITY THE SLAVE Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cnus of all uch as are appointed to destruction Pity the slave the poor degraded slave Deprived of all the rights his Maker gave Forevermore downtrodden and oppressed He has no refuge and no place of rest Until he finds it in the quiet grave Pity the slave the poor degraded slave Pity the slave To woman 1 appeal O let your sympathies be roused to feel The deep the unimaginable woes The bitter untold agony of those Whom cruel man has bound by unjust laws Making it criminal to plead the cause Of the oppressed the outcast and the dumb Or take the weary wanderer to his home Oh is there none who dare attempt to save The poor oppressed despised degraded slave Pity the slave To mothers I appeal Look at the smiling babe for whom you feel A mothers deep and yearning tenderness And fancy if you can the deep distress The overwhelming agony of grief That would admit no comforr no relief Should some stern monster from your bosom teai The idol of affection cherished there And into
